M-050.NAME: mtproto-de-backed-edge
M-050.path: mtproto-runtime nginx docker-compose.yml deploy/deploy-on-server.sh deploy/haproxy-phase38.cfg deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml backend/app/core/config.py backend/app/mtproto scripts/phase38-de-mtproto-edge-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-38.xml
M-050.owns: DE-hosted MTProto runtime, RU public TCP 443 SNI router, private RU-to-DE policy API reachability, and live Telegram availability gates.
M-050.purpose: Restore Telegram MTProto availability by routing issued user SNI traffic from the RU public edge to a DE-hosted MTProto runtime while keeping HTTPS on krotpn.xyz available.

exports.ru-sni-router: Public RU TCP 443 router classifies TLS ClientHello SNI and sends web SNI to local nginx fallback or issued MTProto SNI to DE runtime.
exports.de-mtproto-runtime: DE runtime owns the real MTProto fake-TLS service and upstream Telegram DC connectivity.
exports.de-policy-api: Token-protected policy API is reachable only from RU/backend over the private RU-DE path and never from the public internet.
exports.assignment-replay: Existing active assignment SNI policies are replayed to the DE runtime without rotating MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX or MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT.
exports.phase38-smoke: Static and live smoke gates prove routing roles, policy redaction, HTTPS fallback, and Telegram Desktop availability.

edge-policy.policy-1: RU public TCP 443 has exactly one owner: the SNI router.
edge-policy.policy-2: RU web SNI names such as krotpn.xyz and www.krotpn.xyz route to local private nginx HTTPS fallback on 9443.
edge-policy.policy-3: Issued MTProto SNI names under the configured wildcard domain route to the DE MTProto runtime.
edge-policy.policy-4: DE policy API binds privately and requires the existing runtime token or a deploy-generated replacement that is never logged.
edge-policy.policy-5: Existing assignment secrets, salts, SNI names, and tg links must be preserved unless an explicit reissue phase is approved.

constraints.constraint-1: No public exposure of the DE policy API, runtime token, private key, base secret, salt, derived secret, or full proxy link.
constraints.constraint-2: RU HTTPS rollback must remain available before any public 443 ownership change is applied.
constraints.constraint-3: Telegram availability requires operator-live proof with a real Telegram client after DE runtime activation.

planned-execution.step-1: Design the RU SNI router target map for web SNI, wildcard MTProto SNI, and unknown SNI fallback behavior.
planned-execution.step-2: Define DE runtime install, TLS material delivery, firewall, and private policy API reachability.
planned-execution.step-3: Update backend bridge settings so policy apply/revoke/health can target the DE policy API safely.
planned-execution.step-4: Add deterministic local/static smoke coverage and live operator gates.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 local runtime wiring exists, but RU live mtproto_proxy downstream handshakes remain blocked and nginx public 443 rollback keeps web available.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 planning selects DE-backed MTProto runtime plus RU SNI router as the safer production architecture.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution added RU HAProxy SNI router, DE runtime compose/deploy wiring, private policy bind, backend private policy URL guards, secret-preserving deploy env generation, and deterministic smoke/pytest coverage.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Clean VPS deploy found HAProxy lacked low-port bind permission for 443 and the admin 8443 URL collided with the web fallback; fixed by running sni-router with NET_BIND_SERVICE/root and moving web fallback to private 9443 while preserving admin 8443.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: DE runtime follow-up found the policy API healthy on the private bind but mtproto_proxy down because bootstrap still used 127.0.0.1; fixed by deriving bootstrap from POLICY_LISTEN_IP.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Live RU/DE checks passed for public web/admin titles, RU 443 HAProxy ownership, private nginx fallback 9443, public admin 8443, DE listener 443, private policy health, and HAProxy mtproto_de_runtime backend UP.

M-050.hotspot: H-037: DE-backed MTProto edge and RU SNI router can break HTTPS or expose policy/cert material unless routing, private API reachability, and redaction gates are strict.
